highlights:
# Senior Manager, Global Marketing & C&CL Consumption & Purchase Advanced Analytics Intelligence Services
...
The Coca-Cola Company is building a next-generation, AI-powered marketing analytics ecosystem. We are hiring a senior manager to apply machine learning, GenAI, and advanced statistical modeling to large-scale consumer and shopper data. Hybrid work| Global exposure| High leadership visibility Location: Atlanta, Georgia
...
- Develop and apply ML Models, advanced analytics, and GenAI to marketing and commercial use cases.
- Work with large, complex datasets including consumer tracking, household panels, and syndicated retail data.
- Partner with data platform teams to enable scalable, real-time analytics solutions.
- Translate analytical outputs into clear business recommendations for global stakeholders.
...
- Strong experience in marketing, consumer, or shopper analytics, ideally withing CPG or a related industry.
- Hands-on expertise in advanced analytics, statistical modeling, and data analysis
- Experience working with consumer tracking, household panel, and syndicated retail data (e.g. Nielsen, household panels).
- Proficiency in Python, R, SQL, Power BI; experience with Azure ML or similar platforms is a plus.
- Experience applying analytics to real business decisions (Marketing, growth, pricing or optimization).

results.title: Consultant, Audit Data Analytics job at Coca-Cola in Atlanta, GA | Remotepulse
highlights:
Consultant, Audit Data Analytics job at Coca-Cola in Atlanta, GA | Remotepulse

# Consultant, Audit Data Analytics job at Coca-Cola in Atlanta, GA
...
Title: Consultant, Audit Data Analytics
...
The Data Analyst, Internal Audit will be a key technical member of the Data, Strategy, Analytics, and Insights team. The team will play a critical role in providing valuable insights into audits through data, driving the automation and efficiency of the audits, empowering Internal Audit to further use analytics to bring value to the business. This team is responsible for providing insights to the data that enables audit and the Company to understand and manage risks more effectively.
...
The Data Analyst, Internal Audit will support the team through collecting, cleansing, and analyzing data through technical tools. This role will require the ability to tell a story, which connects the data relationships to risks and provides the capability to look at potential risks differently. The candidate should demonstrate strong leadership, curiosity, communication, and problem-solving skills with the ability to interact in a dynamic team environment and maintain accountability for individual performance objectives and goals.
...
Key Duties and Responsibilities
...
- Engage with audit teams and stakeholders to gather, build, and understand business requirements. Bring structure to requests and translate requirements into an analytic approach to enhance audits.
...
- Leverage AI and advanced analytics techniques (e.g., anomaly detection, predictive analytics, pattern recognition, process mining) to enhance audit planning, scoping, and risk assessment
...
- Produce compelling data visualizations to communicate insights and influence outcomes for audits driving automation and efficiencies.
...
- Actively research and advocate adoption of emerging methods and technologies in the data science field, with an eye toward continually advancing capabilities.
...
### Qualifications and Experience
...
- Bachelor's/master's degree in accounting, Management Information Systems (MIS) or equivalent degree/practical experience
- 2+ years of data analytics experience.
- Internal Audit is a plus.
- Demonstrated experience with operational and financial processes.
- Experience with tools, such as, SAP S/4 Hana, SAP Web IDE, Dynamics & Azure Apps, MS Fabric, MS Power Platform Tools, Alteryx, SQL, R, or Python.
- Ability to organize, cleanse, understand, measure, and analyze big data to understand the relationships, inconsistencies, and unknowns.
- Strong attention to detail and organization skills as well as oral and
...
- Iconic & Innovative Brands: Work with one of the world's most recognized and beloved brand portfolios.
- Commitment to Diversity: We are paving the way to create change in
...
industry, and our leadership is committed to diversity, inclusion and belonging.
- Agile & Innovative Software Platforms: Each day, we work to improve our reporting efficiencies and capabilities using top-tier software platforms including Alteryx, Power BI, and Tableau
...
Alteryx, Communication, Data Analysis, Data Science, Data Visualization, Microsoft Power Business Intelligence (BI), Software Applications

results.title: Analyst, Strategic Insights & Tools | Hirevector
highlights:
# Analyst, Strategic Insights & Tools
...
The Coca-Cola Company is a leading beverage company, and they are seeking an Analyst for their Strategic Insights & Tools team. This role involves developing and automating analytics and reporting solutions for the Advanced Hydration division, partnering with various departments to deliver actionable insights and support ad-hoc requests.
...
- Orchestrate the development of a best-in-class business analytics platform by optimizing end-to-end workflows and automating reporting through technologies such as Visual Studio and Python
...
- Develop & Manage Sales/Shopper Data Reporting, combining several different data sources to create One Source of Truth throughout the Advanced Hydration Categories we play in (Sports Drinks, Flavored Water, Premium Water & Hydration Enhancers)
...
- Create Excel Based tools that align with company objectives (ex: Innovation Tracking, Competitive Analysis, Brand Deep-Dives)
...
- Manage Nielsen & Circana Database Hierarchies to ensure accuracy & alignment with the Companies Category Definitions
...
- Provide Training on Sales & Shopper based tools/reports and being an analytics resource across the organization
...
- Strong understanding of Sales, Shopper Panel & Consumer Insights data and how to leverage insights
...
- Support Marketing, Finance & Sales Organization on Ad-Hoc Data Requests
...
- Share pertinent & thorough updates supporting new technology, reporting capabilities, system developments, and enhancements
...
- Bachelor's degree required (Computer Science degree is a plus)
- 0-2 years relevant analytical experience required
- Hybrid model at Atlanta office required (3 days in-office)
- Advanced Excel (with experience presenting data models required) and PowerPoint
- Team player with exceptional leadership, communication, and collaboration skills
- Demonstrated business acumen, attention to detail, and time‑management abilities
- Experience with one or more of the following: Python, SQL, Tableau, Power BI
- Experience building reports using POS and syndicated data sources (e.g., Nielsen, Circana)
- Experience leveraging insights tools and platforms (e.g., Kantar, Mintel, PlacerAI, Euromonitor)
...
- The Coca-Cola Company is a soft drink manufacturer & distributor that makes a variety of soft drinks, including diet coke and regular coke. It was founded in 1892, and is headquartered in Atlanta, Georgia, USA, with a workforce of 10001+ employees. Its website is https://www.coca-colacompany.com/.

I have noticed a common workflow that I've found very useful over the past year: "
...
on a project
...
brainstorming, looking into
...
new topic or concept
...
example workflow could be trying to code a model. LLM 1 might give me a script which I think is fairly good. Now, what I can do next—even before I implement the code—
...
paste this script into LLM 2 to examine the output, give a critique, and maybe identify some blind spots that
...
1 might have missed
...
An important reason this works is because of sufficient neural diversity among each model. I think the worst thing that could happen is if the top models converge to a similar neural configuration. If that happens, their thought patterns or processes would be too similar to think differently or to identify gaps across the neural thought space these models range. Which is why, in the future, a model might not just rely on a single expert dedicated to a task like coding. Instead, a single LLM framework might deploy multiple distinct code-generation experts simultaneously, intentionally utilizing neural diversity within its own system to deliberate, cross-examine, and reach a consensus before delivering the final output. #LLMs #DataScience #MOE #ChatGPT #Claude

results.title: DJ Lewis
highlights:
Director, AI @ Coca-Cola | Founder, Lewis Collective | People. Purpose. Process.
...
Director, AI Implementation at [The Coca-Cola Company](https://www.linkedin.com/company/the-coca-cola-company)
...
I believe most digital transformations fail not because the technology didn’t work — but because nobody brought the people along. People. Purpose. Process. That’s the framework. Technology — AI or otherwise — exists to unlock human potential, improve decision making, and amplify what humans do best. I’ve spent my career proving that. Whether building AI adoption frameworks at Google, growing revenue divisions from scratch, or leading enterprise transformations across Fortune 500 organizations — the moment that means the most is when someone says “I actually get this now.” That’s the win. Everything else is just infrastructure. I’m now Director of AI Implementation for NAOU Marketing at The Coca-Cola Company — helping one of the world’s most iconic consumer brands harness AI so the marketing team can spend more time doing what only humans can do: creating moments of real connection for every person who reaches for a Coke. I also founded Lewis Collective — a fractional advisory practice helping organizations navigate AI implementation and digital transformation. Not just the tools. The adoption. The culture. The change that actually sticks. I am where I am because someone poured into me. Gave me exposure. Created the conditions for me to have the audacity to bet on myself. That debt doesn’t get repaid — it gets paid forward. Through the DMs, the coffee chats, the phone calls, and the formal work with Big Brothers Big Sisters, Emerging 100, LEAD Atlanta, and Outstanding Atlanta. Sharpening the next generation of leaders who will build what I can’t even imagine yet. I also support Blood Cancer United in the fight against a disease that has touched my family. Some battles you fight because you have no choice but to show up. Rooted in Atlanta. Georgia Tech EMBA, December 2026. Atlanta Business Chronicle + Georgia Tech 40 Under 40. When I’m not building, I’m at the gym, deep in an anime series, or with the people who remind me why any of this matters. Everything I build, I build to last.
...
### Director, AI Implementation at [The Coca-Cola Company](https://www.linkedin.com/company/the-coca-cola-company) (Current)
...
Leading AI implementation across Coca-Cola’s North America Operating Unit — translating marketing technology into measurable business outcomes. I sit at the intersection of people, process, and platform: enabling agency partners (WPP, Publicis), activating our MarTech stack (Adobe, Salesforce, Azure), and building the organizational capability to make AI stick — not just land. My work isn’t about the tools. It’s about building the confidence, fluency, and culture that lets great marketers do their best work with AI behind them.
...
Helping enterprise customers solve business critical problems with solutions enabled by Google cloud. Specialized in big data analytics and production grade ai/ml.
...
Google Cloud Platform problem discovery and business critical solution design specific to the Telecommunication, Media, Entertainment, and Gaming industry spanning the United States and Canada. Key focus areas: artificial intelligence, machine learning, and big data analytics.
...
I am thrilled to share that today I joined The Coca Cola Company as Senior Director, Marketing Capabilities and Technology Enablement. 

As the industry continues to change at an unprecedented speed, this role enables excellence in marketing for one of the worlds most iconic brands. I am so excited to be a part of the team and I know we are going to do some really special things together. 

Here’s to new challenges and great possibilities! #TheCokeLife #TCCC #NewRole #MarketingCapabilities #MarTech #Grateful

highlights:
# How Coca-Cola Turned a 138-Year-Old Brand Into an AI-Powered Creative Machine
...
## What started as a classic soda brand became one of the most talked-about generative AI success stories in marketing, and the lessons extend far beyond Coca-Cola.
...
AI was barely out of its infancy
...
ChatGPT had launched just four months earlier
...
-E
...
had been publicly available for less than a year.
...
brands were still watching from the sidelines, unsure
...
AI was a gimmick or a genuine
...
In a move that would become one of the most cited marketing case studies of the decade, the beverage giant launched “Create Real Magic”, a first-of-its-kind platform that gave digital artists and everyday consumers direct access to generative AI tools built on GPT-4 and DALL-E, pre-loaded with decades of Coca-Cola’s most iconic brand assets. The result wasn’t just a viral campaign. It was a proof of concept for what happens when a legacy brand treats AI not as a production tool, but as a creative collaborator handed directly to its audience.
...
Coca-Cola’s AI journey began quietly. In February 2023, the company announced a strategic partnership with OpenAI and management consultancy Bain & Company. The stated goal was broad: to explore how generative AI could transform marketing from end to end.
...
The company had partnered with OpenAI in 2022, before the public had any real awareness of what OpenAI was building. In January 2023, two months before Create Real Magic launched, it deployed ChatGPT internally to its employees.
...
Built exclusively for Coca-Cola by OpenAI and Bain & Company, the platform was the first of its kind to combine the capabilities of two distinct AI systems working in tandem:
...
- GPT-4, which generated human-like text acting as a brainstorming assistant that helped users write creative prompts, poem captions, and concept descriptions.
- DALL-E, which translated those text prompts into visual artwork, generating original images inspired by the user’s input.
...
2023
...
content creation and
...
AI programs,
...
6. Win a seat at the table. Thirty creators would be selected to travel to Coca-Cola’s global headquarters in Atlanta for the Real Magic Creative Academy, a three-day immersive workshop run by Coke’s Global Design and Creative teams alongside OpenAI, where participants would co-create licensed content, digital collectibles, and branded goods using the same AI tools.
...
- The dual-model approach was central to the experience’s quality. GPT-4 wasn’t just decorative; it actively improved user outputs by helping people articulate their creative vision in language that DALL-E could render effectively. This human-AI-AI creative chain produced results noticeably better than users prompting image generators directly.
...
Later, Coca-Cola’s holiday campaign pushed even further using Microsoft Azure to deliver real-time, natural-language conversations across 26 languages, with sub-millisecond performance, powered by multimodal neural voice models fine-tuned for cultural nuances and regional accents. The same Azure infrastructure handled massive traffic spikes via autoscaling container architecture.
...
5. Partnerships accelerate what internal teams cannot. Coca-Cola did not build its own LLM. It partnered with OpenAI for the model, Bain & Company for the strategy, and later Microsoft Azure for infrastructure. This partnership-first approach let Coca-Cola move from concept to launch in weeks, not years a model worth replicating.
...
Coca-Cola’s generative AI strategy is now past the “early experiment” phase. The company has disclosed that AI contributes to nearly a third of its content creation. Its VP of Creative Strategy has publicly described AI as central to a “major marketing transformation.” The organizational structures, partner relationships, and production pipelines built through initiatives like Create Real Magic are now load-bearing parts of how the brand operates.

results.title: [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II | Wfh
highlights:
# [Remote] Senior Generative AI Scientist II
...
Note: The job is a remote job and is open to candidates in USA. Cotiviti is a healthcare technology company focused on delivering better care at lower costs through advanced technology and data analytics. The Senior Generative AI Scientist II will work with a team to solve healthcare issues using machine learning and AI techniques, delivering value through innovative data solutions. Responsibilities • Work with key stakeholders within Research and Development as well as Business Operations, along with Product Management to assess the potential value and risks associated with business problems that have the potential to be solved using machine learning and Artificial Intelligence techniques • Develop an exploratory data analysis approach to verify the initial hypothesis associated with potential Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning use cases • Document your approach, thinking and results in standard approaches to allow other data scientists to collaborate with you on this work • Prepare your final trained model and develop a validation test set for QA • Work with ML Ops/Production operations to deploy your model into production and support them in monitoring model performance • Participate in design sessions to continuously develop and improve the Cotiviti machine learning platform • Participate in other data science initiatives, collaborating with your peers to support their projects • Participate in knowledge sharing sessions to bring new insights and technologies to the team • Provide End to End value-based solutions, including data pipeline, model creation and application for end user consumption • Complete all responsibilities as outlined in the annual performance review and/or goal setting • Complete all special projects and other duties as assigned • Must be able to perform duties with or without reasonable accommodation Skills • Graduate degree in a quantitative discipline such as Computer Science/Engineering, Statistics, Operations Research covering Advanced Statistics, Machine learning and AI • Experience with the latest techniques in natural language processing including transformers, fine-tuning LLMs, measuring/benchmarking and deploying LLMs with tools such as HuggingFace, Langchain, LLAMA/Mistral and OpenAI, vector databases • 5+ years of hands-on data science/AI experience, using typical machine learning and data science tools including pandas, scikit-learn, keras, nltk, and TensorFlow/PyTorch, GPU • Experience building production-grade machine learning deployments on AWS, Azure, or GCP • Experience working with Apache Spark™ and large-scale distributed datasets • Experience communicating technical concepts to non-technical and technical audiences is a plus • Flexibility to work with global teams as well geographically dispersed US based teams • Professional with ability to properly handle confidential information • Ability to work within a matrixed organization • Proficiency in all required skills and competencies above Benefits • Medical • Dental • Vision • Disability • Life insurance coverage • 401(k) savings plans • Paid family leave • 9 paid holidays per year • 17-27 days of Paid Time Off (PTO) per year, depending on specific level and length of service with Cotiviti. Company Overview • Cotiviti enables healthcare organizations to deliver better care at lower cost through advanced technology and data analytics that improve the quality and sustainability of healthcare in the United States. It was founded in 1979, and is headquartered in South Jordan, UT, US, with a workforce of 5001-10000 employees. Its website is Company H1B Sponsorship • Cotiviti has a track record of offering H1B sponsorships, with 165 in 2025, 118 in 2024, 90 in 2023, 102 in 2022, 72 in 2021, 67 in 2020. Please note that this does not guarantee sponsorship for this specific role.

Experienced Data Scientist – AI/Machine Learning Developer – Generative AI Platform Development | Wfhforgeon
...
# Experienced Data Scientist – AI/Machine Learning Developer – Generative AI Platform Development
...
Join arenaflex, and you'll be part of a team that's changing the world one innovation at a time. We are currently seeking a talented and passionate Data Scientist to join our Cloud Technologies Application Platform team and help us build the future of developer experience through cutting-edge artificial intelligence solutions.
...
Are you ready to take your career to the next level with a company that values innovation and excellence? arenaflex is looking for an experienced Data Scientist to join our dynamic team as an AI/Machine Learning Developer. In this role, you will be instrumental in designing and executing our artificial intelligence strategy to elevate our developer experience platform to unprecedented heights.
...
This is a unique opportunity to work at the forefront of generative AI technology, collaborating with multidisciplinary teams to develop innovative solutions that will transform how developers create applications at arenaflex. You'll leverage your expertise in machine learning, deep learning, and large language models to solve complex business challenges and drive measurable impact across the organization.
...
As a Data Scientist at arenaflex, you will be responsible for driving our AI initiatives and contributing to the development of next-generation developer tools. Your key responsibilities will include:
...
Design, develop, and implement machine learning models and algorithms that enhance our developer experience platform, ensuring alignment with arenaflex's overall technology strategy.
...
Build, train, and fine-tune machine learning models, including foundation models and large language models, to address various business problems and improve developer productivity.
...
handling missing values, identifying outliers, evaluating
...
actionable recommendations.
...
Choose appropriate machine learning algorithms, train models, and calibrate hyperparameters to achieve optimal predictive performance and model accuracy.
...
Apply deep expertise in transformer architecture, vector embeddings, and advanced ML techniques to develop solutions that solve complex business challenges.
...
Lead model deployment for experimentation, implementing A/B testing, monitoring model performance, and iterating on solutions based on empirical results.
...
Work closely with AI engineers, software developers, product managers, and other data scientists to deliver end-to-end AI-enabled solutions.
...
Experience with training models or utilizing foundation models and fine-tuning them to meet custom business requirements.
...
Minimum of 5+ years of progressive experience in the software industry with a focused specialization in machine learning and artificial intelligence.
...
Strong understanding and extensive experience with cutting-edge machine learning algorithms, deep learning architectures, statistical modeling, and large language models.
...
- Advanced degree (Master's or PhD) in Data Science, Computer Science, or related field

- Experience applying machine learning techniques specifically in developer productivity or software development lifecycle contexts

- Knowledge of cloud platforms and infrastructure (AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud)

- Familiarity with MLOps practices and deployment pipelines

- Published research or contributions to open-source machine learning projects

- Experience with prompt engineering and LLM optimization techniques
...
If you are passionate about artificial intelligence, machine learning, and want to work on projects that will shape the future of technology, arenaflex is the place for you. We are looking for individuals who bring enthusiasm, dedication, and fresh perspectives to their work – people who are ready to push boundaries and achieve extraordinary results.
